ambiguities.description: CRM purpose and scope (sales, support, success, marketing, combined)
Entity model (leads, contacts, accounts, opportunities, deals, tickets, activities)
Pipeline or lifecycle stages and how records progress between them
Activity tracking (calls, emails, meetings, notes, tasks) and automation triggers
Data ownership and privacy (who sees what, GDPR, opt-in, data retention)
Integration with email, calendar, marketing, support, or accounting systems
Assignment and routing (manual, round-robin, territory-based, skill-based)
Reporting and forecasting (pipeline value, win rate, activity metrics, quotas)
Import and migration (existing contact lists, spreadsheets, other CRM data)
Communication model (email templates, sequences, campaigns, 1:1 outreach)
MVP CRM boundary — what modules and features are in scope

questions.question: What is the primary CRM purpose (sales, support, success, marketing, combined)?
What entities must be tracked (leads, contacts, accounts, deals, tickets)?
What pipeline or lifecycle stages are required?
What activity types must be tracked?
What assignment and routing model is required?
Must this integrate with email, calendar, or marketing tools?
What reporting and forecasting are required?
What data import or migration is needed?

assumptions.assumption: A combined contact and deal tracking model is acceptable for MVP
A simple pipeline with 3-5 stages is sufficient initially
Manual assignment with optional round-robin is acceptable for MVP
Web-based access is sufficient for MVP

suggested_closures.decision: CRM construction will start with contact and deal tracking under a simple pipeline with manual assignment.
suggested_closures.rationale: A focused entity model and conservative workflow reduce initial complexity while preserving principal control over sales or support processes.

seed_tasks.title: Define CRM purpose and scope
seed_tasks.transformation: Document whether CRM is for sales, support, success, marketing, or combined use.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: CRM purpose and scope document exists.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every stakeholder can identify which functions are in scope.

seed_tasks.title: Define entity model
seed_tasks.transformation: Document leads, contacts, accounts, deals, and any custom entities with their fields.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every entity has defined fields and relationships; no entity is ambiguous.

seed_tasks.title: Define pipeline or lifecycle stages
seed_tasks.transformation: Document stage definitions, entry/exit criteria, and authorized transitions.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every stage has clear entry and exit criteria.

seed_tasks.title: Define assignment and routing rules
seed_tasks.transformation: Document how records are assigned, routed, and reassigned.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every record has a clear owner; no record is orphaned.

seed_tasks.title: Define privacy and data ownership policy
seed_tasks.transformation: Document who can see what data, opt-in requirements, and retention rules.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Privacy and data ownership policy exists.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every data type has an access level and a retention rule.

seed_tasks.title: Define MVP CRM boundary
seed_tasks.transformation: Explicitly list modules and features in MVP and what is out of scope.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: MVP scope document exists with explicit inclusions and exclusions.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Scope is achievable and every excluded module is consciously deferred.

residuals.description: CRM purpose and scope are not defined.
Pipeline or lifecycle stages are not decided.
Assignment and routing model is undefined.
Privacy and data ownership policy is undocumented.
